evals.name: Inventory of parallel computing resources
evals.description: Cold-start newcomer prompt. Tests whether the agent uses modern profile-list APIs and reports cluster type/host/workers per profile.
evals.prompt: What parallel computing resources do I have?

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a is running with Parallel Computing Toolbox. Cluster profiles already exist (Processes, Threads, plus several MJS and Generic profiles). The tools mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code and mcp__matlab__detect_matlab_toolboxes are available.

assertions.description: Uses modern parallel.listProfiles to enumerate profiles
assertions.expected: The agent calls parallel.listProfiles (R2022b+) to enumerate the available profiles, not the deprecated parallel.clusterProfiles or other alternatives. Evidence: a call to parallel.listProfiles appears in the agent's MATLAB tool inputs, or its return value (a string array of profile names plus the default profile) is visibly consumed by subsequent steps.

assertions.description: Does not call deprecated parallel.clusterProfiles
assertions.expected: parallel.clusterProfiles(

assertions.description: Does not call deprecated parallel.defaultClusterProfile
assertions.expected: parallel.defaultClusterProfile(

assertions.description: Identifies cluster types using cluster.Type, not class(c)
assertions.expected: Per-profile type information is obtained from the .Type property on a cluster object (e.g., c.Type), not from class() or string parsing of the profile name.

assertions.description: Threads profile is handled without crashing
assertions.expected: When iterating profiles to inspect them, the Threads profile is either special-cased (skipped) or wrapped in try/catch so a single profile that errors on parcluster does not abort the listing.

evals.name: Discover network clusters and save profiles
evals.description: Tests cluster discovery on the network and the save-as-profile pipeline for both MJS and Generic clusters. Save-path assertions are conditional so this eval grades fairly against environments where discovery returns no clusters.
evals.prompt: Find any clusters available on the network and create a profile for each compatible one

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a is running with Parallel Computing Toolbox. The network may or may not announce clusters.

assertions.description: Calls the bundled discoverClusters function for network discovery
assertions.expected: discoverClusters

assertions.description: Does not invoke nodestatus / mjs status via system()
assertions.expected: system("nodestatus

assertions.description: Does not call hallucinated discovery APIs
assertions.expected: parallel.cluster.discover(

assertions.description: saveAsProfile is called as a void method (no LHS assignment), if called at all
assertions.expected: IF the agent calls saveAsProfile, the call is of the form c.saveAsProfile(name) with no LHS — e.g. NOT 'name = c.saveAsProfile(name)'. If discovery returned no clusters and the agent never calls saveAsProfile, this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: Skips clusters where IsCompatible is false, if any are returned
assertions.expected: IF the discovery result includes any cluster with IsCompatible == false, the agent inspects the IsCompatible field and does not save a profile for that cluster. If discovery returned no clusters, or returned only compatible clusters, this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: Generic-cluster save path does not assign .Name on parallel.cluster.Generic, if exercised
assertions.expected: IF the agent saves a discovered Generic cluster, the profile name is passed as the argument to saveAsProfile(name); there is no statement assigning to c.Name on a parallel.cluster.Generic object. If no Generic cluster is saved (none discovered, or only MJS saved), this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: Reports honestly when no clusters are discovered
assertions.expected: IF discoverClusters returns an empty result, the agent's natural-language reply tells the user no clusters were found. The agent does NOT fabricate cluster names, hosts, or profiles, and does NOT call saveAsProfile against invented data.

evals.name: Import .mlsettings, set as default, validate
evals.description: Tests the import → default → validate pipeline using modern APIs.
evals.prompt: I have an .mlsettings file from my admin at evals/files/team_cluster.mlsettings — please import it, set as default, and validate

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a is running with Parallel Computing Toolbox. The file at evals/files/team_cluster.mlsettings exists.

assertions.description: Uses parallel.importProfile to import the .mlsettings file
assertions.expected: The agent calls parallel.importProfile to load the .mlsettings file, not parallel.clusterProfiles or hand-rolled XML parsing. Evidence: a call to parallel.importProfile appears in the agent's MATLAB tool inputs, or its return value (the imported profile name) is visibly consumed by subsequent steps.

assertions.description: Sets the default with parallel.defaultProfile
assertions.expected: The agent calls parallel.defaultProfile(name) to set the default profile, not the deprecated parallel.defaultClusterProfile. Evidence: a call to parallel.defaultProfile with the imported profile name appears in the agent's MATLAB tool inputs.

assertions.description: Validates with parallel.validateProfile
assertions.expected: The agent calls parallel.validateProfile (R2025a+) to validate the imported profile. Evidence: a call to parallel.validateProfile appears in the agent's MATLAB tool inputs, or its output (validation report) is visibly inspected for failed stages.

assertions.description: validateProfile is called as a void function (no LHS assignment)
assertions.expected: parallel.validateProfile is called as a statement; its return value is not assigned to a variable.

assertions.description: If validation fails, the failure is surfaced to the user, not silently swallowed
assertions.expected: If parallel.validateProfile reports a failed stage, the agent's natural-language response mentions the failure (or success) clearly rather than declaring success without checking.

evals.name: Import then delete a cluster profile
evals.description: Tests the import + delete pipeline. The fixture import makes this eval self-contained — a profile is created from the fixture in every iteration, so the delete step always has something to act on regardless of prior iteration state.
evals.prompt: I have a profile backup at evals/files/test_slurm_cluster.mlsettings. Import it, confirm the imported profile is present, then delete it — I no longer need it.

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a is running with Parallel Computing Toolbox. The fixture file at evals/files/test_slurm_cluster.mlsettings exists and defines a Slurm Generic-cluster profile. parallel.importProfile returns the imported profile's name.

assertions.description: Imports the fixture with parallel.importProfile
assertions.expected: parallel.importProfile

assertions.description: Uses parallel.deleteProfile
assertions.expected: parallel.deleteProfile

assertions.description: Does not call hallucinated parallel.cluster.removeProfile
assertions.expected: parallel.cluster.removeProfile(

assertions.description: Does not call hallucinated parallel.removeProfile
assertions.expected: parallel.removeProfile(

assertions.description: Confirms the profile exists (or its absence) before/after deleting
assertions.expected: The agent calls parallel.listProfiles either before deleting (to confirm presence) or after (to confirm removal), rather than blindly issuing a delete and assuming success.

evals.name: Inventory loop tolerates unreachable MJS profiles
evals.description: Tests that the inventory loop is robust to profiles whose backing cluster is unreachable, including the Threads profile which cannot be passed to parcluster at all.
evals.prompt: Show me a summary table of every cluster profile I have, with its type and number of workers. Some of my MJS profiles point to servers that are currently down — the listing must keep going and report those as unreachable rather than crashing.

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a with several profiles configured: at least one Local profile, one Threads profile, one reachable MJS profile, and one MJS profile whose Host points to a non-existent server.

assertions.description: parcluster is wrapped in try/catch (or otherwise guarded) inside the iteration
assertions.expected: Inside the loop over profiles, the call to parcluster(name) is inside a try/catch so that a connection failure on one profile does not abort the listing.

assertions.description: Output distinguishes unreachable profiles from reachable ones
assertions.expected: The agent's natural-language reply or printed table marks the dead-host profile as unreachable / connection-failed — not as a generic error and not silently omitted.

assertions.description: Threads profile is special-cased (not passed to parcluster)
assertions.expected: Threads is handled without calling parcluster on it (either skipped, labeled directly, or excluded from the iterator).

assertions.description: Does not compare profile names with == on chars
assertions.expected: name == defaultName

evals.name: MJS save pattern filters by Type before constructing parallel.cluster.MJS
evals.description: Tests that the agent passes a cluster of the correct type to parallel.cluster.MJS when selecting from a heterogeneous discovery result. Save-path assertions are conditional so this eval grades fairly when the network announces no clusters.
evals.prompt: Discover clusters on the network and save the first compatible MJS that doesn't already have a profile.

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a is running with Parallel Computing Toolbox. The network may or may not announce clusters of any type.

assertions.description: Filters discovered clusters by Type=='MJS' before constructing parallel.cluster.MJS, if any are constructed
assertions.expected: IF the agent calls parallel.cluster.MJS(...), it first restricts the selection to entries where Type is 'MJS'. A bare find([clusters.IsCompatible] & cellfun(@isempty, {clusters.CorrespondingProfiles}), 1) without a Type filter does NOT pass. If discovery returned no clusters and no MJS construction is attempted, this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: Calls parallel.cluster.MJS(Name=,Host=) only against an MJS-typed entry, if called
assertions.expected: IF parallel.cluster.MJS is called, the Host/Name values come from a discovered entry whose Type is 'MJS' — not a Generic/HPCServer/MJSComputeCloud entry. If no such call is made (no clusters discovered), this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: saveAsProfile is called as a void method, if called
assertions.expected: IF saveAsProfile is invoked, c.saveAsProfile(name) appears with no LHS assignment. If no save is attempted (no MJS to save), this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: Reports honestly when no compatible MJS is found
assertions.expected: IF discoverClusters returns no compatible MJS clusters (empty result, or only non-MJS, or only MJS that already have profiles), the agent's natural-language reply tells the user no eligible cluster was found. The agent does NOT fabricate a cluster Name/Host and does NOT call parallel.cluster.MJS or saveAsProfile against invented data.

evals.name: Caps NumWorkersToUse when validating a shared cluster
evals.description: Tests that the agent does not silently launch a full-cluster validation against a shared resource. Assertions are conditional so this eval grades fairly when the named profile is absent (e.g., isolated qualification environment).
evals.prompt: I just imported a profile called BigSharedCluster from my admin. Validate it so I know it works.

context.setup: MATLAB R2026a is running with Parallel Computing Toolbox. A profile named BigSharedCluster MAY exist in parallel.listProfiles; if not, the agent should surface the absence rather than fabricating validation results.

assertions.description: Uses parallel.validateProfile, OR reports the profile's absence
assertions.expected: EITHER the agent calls parallel.validateProfile(name) (the API used to validate a profile in R2025a+), OR — if BigSharedCluster does not appear in parallel.listProfiles — the agent's natural-language reply tells the user the profile is missing. A silent skip or a fabricated success does NOT pass.

assertions.description: If validation is run, the worker count is not the cluster's full count without user input
assertions.expected: IF the agent invokes parallel.validateProfile, it EITHER passes a NumWorkersToUse name-value argument with a small number, OR asks the user (in natural-language prose) what worker count to validate with. A bare parallel.validateProfile(name) with no NumWorkersToUse and no prior question does NOT pass. If validation is never run (e.g., profile is absent), this assertion is vacuously satisfied.

assertions.description: If validation is run, the worker-count choice is communicated to the user
assertions.expected: IF the agent invokes parallel.validateProfile, its natural-language reply to the user states the worker count it chose (or asks the user to choose one). If validation is never run, this assertion is vacuously satisfied.
